title: Authentic Chinese Egg Rolls from a Chinese Person
instructions: In a large skillet or wok, cook the ground pork over medium-high heat until browned and crumbly. Drain excess fat.
Stir in the shredded cabbage, carrots, garlic, and ginger. Sauté until the vegetables are softened and the moisture has mostly evaporated.
Stir in the soy sauce and sesame oil. Remove from heat and let the filling cool completely.
Place an egg roll wrapper on a flat surface in a diamond shape. Place about 2-3 tablespoons of filling in the center.
Fold the bottom corner over the filling, then fold in the side corners. Roll up tightly, sealing the top corner with a dab of water.
Heat vegetable oil in a deep-fryer or heavy pot to 375°F (190°C).
Fry egg rolls in batches for 3 to 5 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden brown and crispy.
Drain on paper towels and serve warm with dipping sauce.
substitutions: Ground chicken or shrimp can be used instead of ground pork.
Use a pre-shredded coleslaw mix to save time on chopping cabbage and carrots.
Tamari can be substituted for soy sauce for a gluten-free filling (ensure wrappers are also GF).
